body.title: Graph-tree embeddings + the teach-on-miss principle (the LLM grows the graph, never answers)
body.summary: THE KEYSTONE COMPILED-INFERENCE PRINCIPLE (Akien, confirmed 2026-06-30): the LLM NEVER produces the answer. It only ever PUSHES NODES into the graph tree, and the graph tree always produces output in its own (node-activation) shape. This guarantees (1) representational COHERENCE — one shape everywhere, no dense-vector-vs-graph mismatch (the exact thing that blocked the embedder repoint); and (2) monotonic CONVERGENCE — every miss makes the graph permanently better, so the LLM-call rate declines over time and the system learns its way off the LLM. First application = embeddings: text -> the SET of activated word-graph concept-nodes (the 'embedding'); similarity / LIKE = NODE-OVERLAP (Jaccard / path-distance), NOT cosine. Nomic dense vectors retire ENTIRELY; ALL current embedding consumers (valence anchors, kNN, factual_compression, distillation, narrative_engine, cortex memory_embeddings) get redefined in node-overlap terms. On a MISS (text activates too few nodes / low confidence, or a LIKE search returns nothing plausible), the LLM is invoked NOT to answer (that would be a different shape) but to PUSH the missing nodes WITH a checkable target ('after these nodes, this text should land near memories X,Y'); the pushed nodes COMMIT ONLY IF the graph then actually produces that target -> proof-on-write, the graph-write analogue of proof-on-close. teach-on-miss SUPERSEDES the earlier 'LLM-compare-for-training' framing (the LLM stops producing a rival embedding to compare; it just teaches). Node MERGE is required (living-entities discipline at the node level) so repeated misses on overlapping concepts merge into existing nodes, not duplicate. Convergence SIGNAL: teach-call-rate over time must decline. Affordable now because inference is free (Ollama Cloud today, Hex within hours).

key_concepts.name: LLM-teaches-never-answers
key_concepts.what: the universal move: on a miss the LLM writes nodes into the graph; the graph always produces the answer in its native shape. Generalizes beyond embeddings to any graph-tree capability.

key_concepts.name: node-activation embedding
key_concepts.what: text -> set of activated concept-nodes; LIKE/similarity = node-overlap, not a dense vector. This is what makes embeddings 'simple enough to do in graph trees'.

key_concepts.name: proof-on-write gate
key_concepts.what: LLM-pushed nodes must come with a checkable target and commit only if the graph then produces it; provenance-tag pushed nodes. Prevents compiling confabulation into the graph (a bad node is WORSE than a bad answer — it's permanent).

key_concepts.name: teach-on-miss supersedes compare
key_concepts.what: no rival-embedding comparison; the miss IS the training signal and the teach IS the update.

key_concepts.name: node merge (living entities)
key_concepts.what: repeated misses merge into existing nodes; same compete/merge/decompose discipline as intentions, one level down.

key_concepts.name: convergence metric
key_concepts.what: teach-call-rate declines over time = the architecture works; flat/rising = graph not retaining or domain genuinely open-ended.

key_concepts.name: nomic full retirement
key_concepts.what: all embedding uses move to node-overlap; delete embedder.py (nomic/Ollama), the file cache (~/.unseen_university/cache/embeddings/), and the Postgres memory_embeddings table.

implementing_files: TBD — graph-tree embedder existence UNVERIFIED (a prior Sonnet claimed it was built; our investigation found only the stale Scraps embedding_engine, which is the OLD version). Verify first.
body.status_notes: Principle CONFIRMED by Akien 2026-06-30; tickets filed under D-graph-tree-embeddings-teach-on-miss-2026-06-30, specified to be Haiku-buildable (dogfood of the dev-pipeline: CC specs, cheap worker builds). Embedder existence must be VERIFIED before build (do not trust the 'it's built' claim).
